BURN_SEVERE_001.name: Severe Thermal Burn
steps: Hold the burned area under cool (not cold) running water for 10-20 minutes immediately. Do not use ice.
Do NOT apply ice, butter, toothpaste, oil, or any cream to the burn.
Remove any jewelry or tight clothing near the burn BEFORE swelling starts.
Cover the burn loosely with a clean non-fluffy cloth or clear cling film.
Do not pop blisters. Seek emergency medical care immediately.

BURN_MINOR_002.name: Minor Burn First Degree
steps: Cool the area immediately with cool (not cold) running water for at least 10 minutes.
Apply a gentle soothing lotion or aloe vera gel once the area has cooled completely.
Cover with a loose sterile non-adhesive bandage or clean cloth.
Take paracetamol for pain relief if needed — do NOT take ibuprofen on a burn.
Monitor for signs of infection over the next 24-48 hours (pus, increasing redness, swelling).

CHOKING_ADULT_001.name: Adult Choking
steps: Ask the person: Are you choking? If they cannot speak, cough, or breathe — act immediately.
Give 5 firm back blows: lean person forward, use heel of your hand between shoulder blades.
Give 5 abdominal thrusts (Heimlich): stand behind, make fist above navel, pull sharply inward and upward.
Alternate 5 back blows then 5 abdominal thrusts until the object dislodges or they lose consciousness.
If they become unconscious: lay them down, call ambulance immediately, and begin CPR.

FRACTURE_001.name: Suspected Fracture or Broken Bone
steps: Tell the person to stay still. Do NOT attempt to move or straighten the injured limb.
Immobilize the limb in the position found: use a splint (rolled magazine, board, umbrella) tied gently above and below the injury.
Apply a cold pack or ice wrapped in cloth — 20 minutes on, 20 minutes off — to reduce swelling.
If the bone is protruding through skin (open fracture): cover with a clean sterile cloth, do not push it in.
Keep the person warm and as still as possible. Call ambulance or arrange urgent hospital transport.

CARDIAC_001.name: Cardiac Arrest or Unresponsive Person
steps: CALL AMBULANCE (108/112) IMMEDIATELY — do this before anything else.
Check response: tap shoulders firmly and shout loudly. If no response and not breathing normally, begin CPR.
CPR — chest compressions: place heel of hand center of chest, push down 5-6cm, 100-120 compressions per minute.
After every 30 compressions, give 2 rescue breaths: tilt head, lift chin, pinch nose, breathe into mouth for 1 second.
Continue until ambulance arrives, the person recovers, or an AED becomes available. Use AED as soon as possible.

BLEEDING_001.name: Severe External Bleeding
steps: Apply firm direct pressure to the wound immediately using a clean cloth or bandage — do not remove once applied.
If blood soaks through, add more layers on top — do NOT remove the first layer.
Raise the injured area above heart level if possible (unless broken bone suspected).
For limb bleeding that will not stop: apply a tourniquet 5cm above the wound, note the time applied.
Keep the person warm and still. Call ambulance for severe or uncontrolled bleeding.

MINOR_WOUND_001.name: Minor Cut or Wound
steps: Rinse the wound gently under clean running water for 1-2 minutes to remove any debris.
Apply gentle pressure with a clean cloth or bandage until bleeding stops — this usually takes 5-10 minutes.
Clean around the wound with mild soap, avoiding getting soap inside the wound.
Apply an antiseptic cream and cover with a sterile adhesive bandage.
Monitor for signs of infection over the next 24-48 hours: increasing redness, swelling, warmth, or pus.

SEIZURE_001.name: Seizure or Convulsion
steps: Stay calm. Time the seizure. Clear away any hard or sharp objects from around the person.
Do NOT restrain the person. Do NOT put anything in their mouth.
Cushion the person's head with something soft. Loosen any tight clothing around the neck.
When shaking stops, gently roll the person onto their side (recovery position) to keep airway clear.
Stay with them until they are fully alert. Call ambulance if it is their first seizure, lasts over 5 minutes, or they are injured.

POISONING_001.name: Poisoning or Toxic Ingestion
steps: Call Poison Control (1800-11-6117 India) or emergency services (108) IMMEDIATELY.
Do NOT induce vomiting unless explicitly told to do so by Poison Control.
If the person is unconscious and not breathing normally, begin CPR.
Keep the container or packaging of the substance — tell emergency services exactly what was ingested.
Stay with the person and monitor their breathing and consciousness until help arrives.

DEFAULT_001.name: General First Aid
steps: Stay calm and assess the situation — your composure helps the injured person.
Ensure the scene is safe before approaching — do not put yourself at risk.
Call emergency services (108) if the situation seems life-threatening or you are unsure.
Keep the person comfortable, still, and warm. Do not give food or water unless they are fully conscious.
Monitor their breathing and level of consciousness until professional help arrives.
